customers: Claims 1,000+ technology companies and research institutions across autonomous driving, generative AI, and embodied AI. Named/cited references include Meta, TikTok, and Pika (as data clients) and Mech-Mind Robotics (embodied-AI partner); the broader paying roster is otherwise undisclosed.
revenue_status: early-revenue (~$9.5M revenue/ARR 2025 per getLatka third-party estimate, unverified; ~$28.4M valuation, unverified); raised an $8M round to expand the data-infrastructure platform.
technical_differentiation: Differentiates on a unified multimodal data-engineering platform (MooreData) with built-in AI automation claiming 500-1000% throughput gains plus consensus labeling, sampling audits, automated error detection, and a dedicated embodied-AI/robot pipeline—versus larger horizontal labeling rivals Scale AI and Surge AI, where Abaka competes on automation-heavy tooling, flexible on-prem/hybrid deployment, and lower-cost APAC-rooted operations rather than scale or capital.

one_liner: Managed teleoperation-as-a-service for robots: sub-40ms streaming software plus trained remote operators that handle edge cases while capturing synced video, telemetry and operator input as training data.
notes: Recently out of stealth (late 2025; announced via LinkedIn ~Nov 2025). Founder/CEO: James Naylor. HQ San Francisco (2261 Market St) with a London, UK office (Paddington Works). Listed as a portfolio startup of F4 Fund; specific funding round, size, lead, and date are not publicly disclosed (Crunchbase/PitchBook list it as private, 1-10 employees, founded 2025). Product: hardware-agnostic teleop platform (ROS/ROS2, AES-256 E2E encryption, SOC2-targeted) for humanoids, robot arms, AMRs, and AVs, with a managed/recruited operator network. Per layer assignment: classed as L3 Data Infrastructure (teleop/demonstration-data collection service core to robot learning) with L6/6h_other secondary. in_scope=true: teleoperation/demonstration-data services are an AI-native enabling category for learned robot policies. Also serves AV teleop, but it is a robot-teleop data-infra vendor, not a pure AV company, so the AV exclusion does not disqualify it. Founded year and one-liner sourced from adamohq.com and Crunchbase/PitchBook profiles.
business_model: Sells "Teleop," a sub-40ms glass-to-glass teleoperation streaming platform (ROS/ROS2-native, multi-path LTE/5G/Wi-Fi bonding), bundled with a fully managed service of vetted, trained remote operators who handle robot edge cases and capture synced demonstration data. Buyers are robotics/AV/humanoid companies; sold as a combined software subscription plus managed-operator service (24/7 fleet coverage), with pricing not publicly disclosed (likely per-operator/per-hour or per-robot fleet contracts).
customers: Cavalla (warehouse-automation robotics company, founder Mohammad Nafisi) is featured by Adamo as a customer/partner; the relationship is referenced but not confirmed as paying. No other named customers are publicly disclosed.
revenue_status: pre-revenue (no public revenue or ARR figure; founded 2025, 1-10 employees, came out of stealth late 2025, backed by F4 Fund; early/pilot stage)
technical_differentiation: Differentiates by pairing ultra-low-latency (sub-40ms) streaming with bonded multi-path connectivity and a fully managed, psychometrically vetted remote-operator workforce plus synced data capture, versus software-only or platform-only competitors. Closest named competitors: Formant (fleet ops/observability software, but not a managed-operator service) and Phantom Auto (pioneer of remote-operation software, now shut down) - Adamo's combined software-plus-staffed-operators model is its main edge over these.

also_known_as: Songling Robotics; AgileX; Lobot; 松灵机器人（深圳）有限公司
one_liner: Builds Pika universal embodied-AI data collectors: handheld + gripper end-effectors capturing synchronized 6D pose, RGB, depth, IMU and gripper-state data to train robot manipulation policies.
notes: 松灵机器人 = AgileX Robotics, founded 2016 by Wei Jidong (ex-DJI), based in Shenzhen/Dongguan, Guangdong, China (legal entity 松灵机器人（深圳）有限公司; originated in Dongguan Songshan Lake Xbot Park). Core business is mobile robot chassis; Pika is its embodied-AI data-collection product line. Pika modules: Pika Sense (handheld collector), Pika Gripper (robotic end-effector, ±1.5mm spatial positioning, up to 2kg grip), Pika Ego/Pro (egocentric). Outputs 6D pose, RGB, depth, IMU, gripper angle via Type-C. Investors include 5Y Capital, HSG/Sequoia China, Vertex Ventures China, Legend Star, Xbot Park. Total funding reported ~$15.4-15.5M; most prominent round Series A (Aug 2021); later early-stage VC activity reported but round size/lead undisclosed. Assigned L3 Data Infrastructure with L4 hardware secondary since Pika is physical capture hardware. Team size not reliably found.
business_model: Hardware sales of robotics R&D products: mobile robot chassis/cobots/arms and, for Layer 3 data infrastructure, the Pika line (PiKA Sense, PiKA Gripper, PiKA Station) sold as modular kits or bundled packages capturing synchronized 6D pose, RGB, depth, IMU and gripper-state for imitation-learning/behavior-cloning datasets. Sold direct and via global distributors (Generation Robots, OpenELAB, Robots International) at list prices around EUR 15,600 per component up to EUR 62,400 for a full Pika package.
customers: Primarily academic and corporate embodied-AI research labs purchasing through distributors; no specific named paying customers are publicly disclosed. Undisclosed.
revenue_status: undisclosed (no public revenue/ARR figure; has raised ~$15.5M incl. a ~RMB 100M Series A from Sequoia Capital China, 5Y Capital, Vertex Ventures China, HKX Technology Fund — unverified)
technical_differentiation: Productizes the academic UMI (Universal Manipulation Interface, Stanford) handheld gripper concept into a commercial, ruggedized kit adding active depth, IMU and precise 6D pose with a docking Station for synchronized multimodal capture, offering turnkey ease vs. DIY/open-source UMI builds. Competes with Galaxea/other Chinese embodied-AI data-collection vendors largely on integration, accuracy (+/-1.5mm) and distribution/execution.

one_liner: Gig-workforce data provider that captures 1,000+ hours/day of 4K head-mounted egocentric POV video across homes, retail, and factories to train embodied-AI and humanoid robot manipulation policies.
total_funding_usd: ~52M (approx $27.5M pre-Series C plus $24.5M Mynavi Series C)
notes: Parent company Awign is a Bengaluru gig-workforce / work-as-a-service (HRtech) platform with 1.5M+ gig workers across 1,000+ cities, founded 2016 by Annanya Sarthak, Gurpreet Singh, and Praveen Kumar Sah. Japan's Mynavi Corporation acquired a majority stake (~73%) via a $24.5M Series C in April 2024, making Awign a Mynavi subsidiary (hence status acqui-hired / division). The robotics-relevant offering is its "Robotech Egocentric" data product, which delivers high-volume 4K first-person head-mounted demonstration video (reported 1,000+ hours/day) for imitation learning and embodied AI, sold to robotics labs/companies. Scope: borderline — the core business (gig-staffing) exists without AI, but the specific census-assigned product is a robot-training human-demonstration data service, which is AI-native (would not exist without robot learning), so in_scope=true. Total funding: sources report ~$27.5M-$28.85M over 9 rounds before the Series C; adding the $24.5M Mynavi round gives ~$52M (approximate; some figures may overlap). Last-round data verified via Entrackr, YourStory, Mynavi press release. Team size as a standalone employee count not reliably findable.
business_model: Sells outcome-based gig-workforce execution to enterprises (auditing, proctoring, last-mile, data/content ops) on a per-task/contract basis via its no-code "work-as-a-service" platform powered by 1.5M+ gig partners. The Layer 3 line ("Egocentric Data for Robotics") productizes that same gig network to capture and deliver 1,000+ hours/day of 4K head-mounted egocentric POV video (homes/retail/factories) as training datasets sold to robotics/embodied-AI labs; specific per-hour or licensing pricing is not publicly disclosed.
customers: Core gig-workforce clients (175+ enterprises) include named orgs Infosys, Mahindra & Mahindra, Tata Group, Swiggy, Britannia, and upGrad. Buyers specifically for the egocentric robotics-video data product are not publicly disclosed.
revenue_status: scaling (₹204Cr / ~$24M revenue FY2025, ending Mar 31 2025; reported ~10x revenue growth over two years) — figure is for the overall gig-workforce business; egocentric-data line revenue not broken out. Acquired by Japan's Mynavi (73% stake, all-cash) in April 2024.
technical_differentiation: Differentiates on a 1.5M+ gig-worker network across 1,000+ Indian cities enabling unusually high-volume, low-cost capture (1,000+ hrs/day 4K egocentric via head-mounted iPhones/GoPros) with demographic/environmental diversity. Vs. named egocentric-data competitors Objectways and Appen (plus newer entrants Build AI, Lightwheel), its edge is raw collection scale and price rather than annotation tooling; on annotation/QA it is largely undifferentiated and competes on execution and gig-labor cost arbitrage.

one_liner: Collects large-scale egocentric video from factory workers via custom head-mounted glasses to scale robot manipulation learning; released open-source Egocentric-10K dataset (10,000+ hours).
team_size: unknown (small team; "five dudes in one room" early stage)
notes: Founded April 2025 by Eddy Xu (CEO, 18-yo Columbia dropout) and Jonathan Jia (CTO). Mission: "collecting human data to accelerate the deployment of general-purpose robots." Builds custom head-mounted recording glasses deployed in real factories; manufacturing/iteration operations in Shenzhen, China, founded/based in San Francisco. Released Egocentric-10K on Hugging Face (Apache 2.0): ~10,000 hours, 1.08B frames, 2,153 workers, 1080p/30fps monocular head-mounted video covering machining, sorting, assembly, packaging, inspection. Later expanded to Egocentric-100K (100,000+ hrs, 14,228 workers) with stated roadmap to 1M hours by April 2026. Seed round $5M (2025) from Abstract Ventures, Pear VC, HF0; angels include ZFellows and Alexandra Botez. in_scope=true: borderline human-video data play but explicitly built for robot manipulation learning (AI-native demonstration-data service). last_round_date precise month not confirmed; year only. Team size not disclosed.
business_model: Pre-revenue venture-backed startup ($5M seed from Pear VC, Abstract Ventures, HF0). It manufactures custom head-mounted recording glasses, deploys them with factory-worker networks (notably across Southeast Asia) to collect egocentric "economically useful human data," and currently open-sources the resulting datasets (Egocentric-10K, Egocentric-100K on Hugging Face under Apache 2.0). No paid product or licensing model is publicly disclosed; the likely future model is selling/licensing proprietary egocentric data and foundation models to robotics labs and humanoid developers.
customers: Undisclosed. Datasets are released open-source (Apache 2.0) and used by AI/robotics researchers and labs, but no named paying or piloting customers are publicly disclosed.
revenue_status: pre-revenue
technical_differentiation: Differentiates on scale and authenticity of real-world egocentric data: captures workflows from thousands of real factory workers (2,153 for the 10K dataset; 14,228 for the 100K, 100,000+ hours / ~25TB) using its own custom monocular 128-degree 1080p/30fps glasses in live industrial settings, claiming the largest egocentric dataset ever. Versus academic egocentric efforts like Meta's Ego4D/Ego-Exo4D and lab human-data methods like Stanford's EMMA, Build AI is positioned as the first company exclusively scaling "economically useful" production-environment egocentric data via its own deployed hardware fleet rather than academic crowdsourcing.

one_liner: Teleoperation- and egocentric-video data-as-a-service for physical AI: a managed global collector network gathers robot demonstration data delivered in RLDS/HDF5/Zarr for ACT, Diffusion Policy and VLA training.
team_size: ~12 (11-50 per LinkedIn) core team, plus 10,000+ contract data collectors
notes: Spinout from Moonvalley (LA-based AI video company, ~$154M raised); founded by Moonvalley co-founders John Thomas, Chad Birdsall, Oni Baballari, built from Moonvalley's data-capture pipeline. 100% focused on physical AI / robotics training data: teleoperation collection stations (ALOHA-style leader-follower, SpaceMouse, multi-view cameras) producing 1,000+ demonstrations/week; also 500K+ egocentric videos and 4M+ annotations; data delivered in RLDS/HDF5/Zarr with URDF, camera intrinsics/extrinsics, action-space metadata. In_scope=true: AI-native demonstration-data service that only exists to train learned manipulation policies. HQ city (Los Angeles) inferred from Moonvalley parent — not independently confirmed for Claru; country US is high-confidence. Founding year not published (recent spinout, likely 2025); funding not disclosed. Verification=incomplete because founded_year lacks a confirming source. Sources: https://claru.ai/about, https://claru.ai/solutions/teleoperation-data, https://www.linkedin.com/company/claru-ai, https://www.crunchbase.com/organization/claru
business_model: Data-as-a-service: Claru sells custom training-ready robotics datasets (teleoperation/manipulation demonstrations, large-scale egocentric video, VLA fine-tuning data) collected via a managed global network of 10,000+ trained collectors across 100+ cities. Buyers are frontier AI/robotics labs; engagements are per-project/dataset contracts ("brief to first delivery in days"), with 100+ active licensed datasets, though specific per-clip or contract pricing is not publicly disclosed.
customers: Not publicly named. Claru references "5+ frontier lab partnerships" with leading AI research teams training world models, VLAs, and video-generation systems, but discloses no individual customer names (undisclosed).
revenue_status: early-revenue (no Claru-specific revenue/ARR figure publicly disclosed; $5M revenue figure found pertains to founders' prior company Moonvalley, not Claru)
technical_differentiation: Differentiates on a 100%-physical-AI focus with an owned global collector network (10,000+ collectors, 100+ cities) delivering licensed, real-human egocentric + on-hardware teleoperation data (ALOHA-style leader-follower, multi-view rigs on Franka/UR5e/ViperX) rather than synthetic or scraped data. Versus general data-labeling vendors like Scale AI and Defined.ai (which Claru explicitly positions against), it specializes in robotics demonstration capture and physical-AI annotation depth instead of broad NLP/vision labeling.

one_liner: Decentralized blockchain protocol that crowdsources, annotates and verifies human-generated data including robot trajectory datasets, paying contributors on-chain royalties while AI and robotics firms pay for access.
notes: Decentralized, multi-chain data protocol (native token XNY). Curates crowdsourced, human-generated data assets; explicitly targets robotics as a vertical, building a human-centric robot-data ecosystem for real-world deployment with contributors earning perpetual on-chain royalties and AI/robotics firms paying for dataset access. Qualifies as L3 Data Infrastructure with a specific robotics-data product (open robotic data at scale). Founded 2023 (PitchBook/Tracxn); co-founders Yi Zhang (CEO), Paul Pang (CTO), Kevin Wang (CPO). HQ Singapore, ~13 employees. $2.5M seed led by OKX Ventures (formerly OKX Blockdream Ventures), announced 2024-06-12, with Comma3 Ventures, Mask Network, CGV, CatcherVC, Fibo Partners and others. in_scope=true: robot demonstration/trajectory data services are AI-native (no equivalent without learned policies). Note the protocol is general data infrastructure (healthcare, finance, food, robotics) not robotics-exclusive; robotics is one served vertical. Total funding reflects only the disclosed seed round; other rounds undisclosed. Sources: pitchbook.com/profiles/company/606205-27, coincarp.com/fundraising/codatta-seed/, crypto-fundraising.info/projects/codatta/.
business_model: Decentralized data protocol that crowdsources, annotates and verifies human/expert data (e.g. its Crypto Account Annotation System covering 35 chains and 46M flagged addresses, plus robotics trajectory datasets) and sells access to AI developers via a blockchain-based "data assetization" royalty model: buyers pay no upfront fee and instead share revenue/royalties (in fiat, stablecoin, or the XNY token) tied to ongoing dataset usage; contributors earn perpetual royalties. The XNY token also funds payments, staking, and governance.
customers: Data is reportedly used to train open-source LLMs including Alibaba Cloud's Qwen; integrations with AI-agent ecosystems such as Kite AI; first project selected for the Binance Booster Program. Targets AI developers/startups and Web2 labelling platforms (MTurk, Scale AI). No named paying enterprise contracts publicly disclosed; most "customers" are ecosystem/usage relationships rather than confirmed paid deals.
revenue_status: early-revenue (no public revenue/ARR figure; raised $2.5M seed June 2024 led by OKX Ventures)
technical_differentiation: Versus centralized data-labeling leaders Scale AI and Amazon Mechanical Turk, Codatta differentiates via a permissionless, token-incentivized contributor network (100k+ contributors) with on-chain provenance and perpetual royalties instead of one-time piece-rate pay. Versus other decentralized data/DePIN players (e.g. Sahara AI, Vana), its edge is a proven verticalized dataset (the CAA crypto-risk annotation system, 560M annotations) extending into robotics trajectory data.

one_liner: Builds large-scale real-world workplace robot and egocentric datasets for embodied AI via a marketplace where workplaces get paid to host egocentric-capture and robot-teleop sessions.
notes: L3 Data Infrastructure: robotics-specific trajectory/demonstration data curation. Founded 2025 by Lucas Ngoo (Carousell co-founder/ex-CTO). YC Fall 2025 batch. Raised $6M seed (Dec 2025); 500 Global is named lead/investor per multiple sources (Tracxn cites 500 Global; LinkedIn posts from 500 Global and Tech in Asia confirm $6M). Builds egocentric human video (hand/body pose, depth, subtask labels) + robot trajectories from manipulators/humanoids in real industry settings + human-in-the-loop teleop rollouts. Operates Cortex Marketplace where workplaces are paid to host capture/teleop sessions. in_scope=true: demonstration-data service for robot learning, AI-native category. NOT in census per assignment.
business_model: Two-sided data marketplace: real workplaces are paid to host egocentric/robot data-collection and human-in-the-loop evaluation sessions, and Cortex sells the resulting in-the-wild datasets (egocentric human video with hand/body pose, depth, subtask labels; robot manipulator/humanoid trajectories) plus rollout/eval services to robotics buyers. Buyers are frontier robotics foundation-model labs and humanoid companies; pricing/contract terms (per-dataset, per-trajectory, or subscription) are not publicly disclosed.
customers: Undisclosed. Publicly stated target buyers are frontier robotics foundation-model research groups and humanoid robot companies, but no named paying or piloting customers have been disclosed.
revenue_status: pre-revenue (raised ~$6M seed, YC F25; no public revenue/ARR figure)
technical_differentiation: Differentiates from data-labeling/annotation incumbents like Encord and Sama by sourcing proprietary real-workplace, in-the-wild egocentric and robot-trajectory data via a paid marketplace of physical worksites (plus a human-in-the-loop recovery/eval flywheel), rather than annotating client-supplied data; the marketplace-supply moat leverages CEO Lucas Ngoo's prior C2C marketplace experience (Carousell).

company_name: DataOcean AI (Beijing Haitian Ruisheng Technology Co., Ltd.)
also_known_as: 海天瑞声; Haitian Ruisheng; Haitianruisheng; SH:688787
one_liner: Listed AI training-data provider that built an embodied-intelligence data training field collecting real teleoperation/demonstration data across humanoid, multi-arm and quadruped robots for robot-learning developers.
total_funding_usd: undisclosed (public company, STAR Market IPO Aug 2021)
last_round_type: IPO (STAR Market / SSE 科创板, SH:688787)
notes: Founded 2005 in Beijing (Haidian District); one of China's earliest AI training-data providers; IPO on Shanghai STAR Market (科创板) under code 688787 on Aug 13, 2021. Rebranded English name to "DataOcean AI" in May 2025. Legacy business is speech/CV/NLP data labeling (out of robotics scope), but qualifies via dedicated embodied-AI offering: in Aug 2025 it co-launched a '具身智能数据训练场' (embodied-intelligence data training field) with the Shijingshan humanoid robot data training center (Shougang Park) and released what it calls China's first embodied-AI data engineering service platform. Collects real interaction data across single/multi-arm, humanoid, and quadruped bodies using head-ring/gripper rigs, mocap suits/gloves, and teleoperation cockpits — a teleoperation/demonstration data service, hence in_scope=true under L3 Data Infrastructure. 2024 revenue ~RMB 237M (+39%), net profit ~RMB 11.2M (returned to profitability). Team size not disclosed in summaries reviewed. Funding/round-size figures undisclosed beyond the public STAR Market listing. Assigned primary layer L3 with L6/6h_other secondary per task.
business_model: Sells AI training-data products and services on a per-project / dataset-license basis: data collection, annotation, design, and model evaluation across speech, computer vision, and NLP, plus ~1,800 off-the-shelf licensable datasets in 190+ languages/dialects. Buyers are AI/large-model developers and autonomous-driving/robotics firms; 2025 expansion added an embodied-intelligence "data training field" collecting real teleoperation/demonstration data sold as end-to-end robotics datasets. Gross margins historically >70%.
customers: Named clients include Alibaba, Tencent, Baidu, ByteDance, iFlytek (科大讯飞), Hikvision, Microsoft, Amazon, and Samsung; cumulative customers exceed 1,200, with 70+ named autonomous-driving clients.
revenue_status: early-revenue (FY2024 revenue ~RMB 237M / ~$33M, +40% YoY, net profit RMB 11.3M turning profitable; H1 2025 revenue RMB 157M / ~$22M, +69.5% YoY with net profit +813.65%)
technical_differentiation: Only A-share-listed pure-play AI training-data provider; differentiates on breadth (190+ languages/dialects, ~1,800 standardized datasets) and a dedicated embodied-intelligence data-training field for real teleoperation/demo data, versus closest Chinese competitors Datatang (数据堂) and Testin/Yunce Data (云测数据), which lack public-market scale and the multilingual + robotics data-factory depth.

also_known_as: 数据堂; Datatang (Beijing) Technology Co., Ltd.; 数据堂(北京)科技股份有限公司
one_liner: AI training-data provider that built an 8,000 sqm real-scene data-collection factory to capture physical-world robot demonstrations, supplying real-machine embodied-intelligence training data plus collection/annotation services.
notes: Founded 2011, HQ Beijing (Kemao Tower). Listed on China's NEEQ / New Third Board (新三板, stock code 831428) since Dec 2014 — first AI-data-service company on NEEQ; as of Jan 2024 in IPO tutoring to move to the Beijing Stock Exchange (北交所), so status=ipo (publicly listed). Total funding and team size not publicly disclosed. In_scope=true: the embodied real-scene data-collection factory (8,000 sqm 实景数采工厂) is a robotics real-machine demonstration-data product that is AI-native (exists to train embodied-intelligence/VLA manipulation policies), not generic data labeling. Datatang itself does not run learned robot policies; it is a Layer 3 data-infrastructure supplier. Secondary L6 tag reflects the vertical robotics-data product (6h_other). Core business also covers autonomous-driving, speech, CV and NLP data, but those are out of the robotics-policy scope.
business_model: Sells AI training data via three lines: licensed off-the-shelf datasets (speech, image/video, text, biometric, autonomous-driving), custom data collection/annotation projects, and data-processing solutions, priced as per-project contracts and dataset licenses billed to AI/tech buyers. Newer push: an ~8,000 sqm real-scene "data factory" capturing real-machine robot demonstration data for embodied-AI customers.
customers: Named top-five clients (2022 disclosures) include Baidu and Tencent, plus autonomous-driving firm Momenta (毫末智行, ~14% of sales) and Phantom AI (~5.8%); company stopped disclosing specific customer names after 2022.
revenue_status: scaling (¥236M revenue FY2023, +39% YoY, net profit ¥39.7M; H1 2024 revenue ¥88.8M, +45% YoY)
technical_differentiation: Differentiates on scale of accumulated licensed data assets (~800TB image/video, 200,000 hrs speech) and 5,000+ in-house annotators across multiple bases, versus pure-play data labelers like Appen and Scale AI; in embodied-AI data collection it is one of several Chinese entrants (alongside AgiBot/智元's 4,000 sqm factory and Pasini/帕西尼's super-factory) and is not clearly differentiated there - competing on execution and existing data infrastructure.

also_known_as: Shenzhen Elephant Robotics Technology Co., Ltd.
one_liner: Builds the myController S570 portable exoskeleton that captures bimanual motion data at 100Hz for teleoperation and end-to-end imitation-learning training of cobots and its Mercury humanoid.
notes: Founded 2016, Shenzhen; incubated at HAX (SOSV) with SOSV seed funding. Qualifying L3 data-infra product is the myController S570 portable exoskeleton controller — a wearable 12-DOF (6 per arm), 570mm reach teleop rig that captures motion data at 100Hz, integrates with LeRobot, ROS1/2 and Python, and feeds end-to-end imitation-learning pipelines; also used as the exoskeleton to teleoperate/collect training data for its Mercury X1/humanoid. Company also sells generic cobots (myCobot, mechArm, etc.) and the Mercury humanoid — hence L4_hardware/L6 secondary tagging. In-scope: the data-capture/teleoperation-for-training-data category is AI-native (exists to feed learned policies). Most recent round ~2024-05; round classification conflicts across sources (Crunchbase lists a Series B-labeled URL, others Series A/Pre-B), lead Qinghui Investment with Yunzhuo Capital participating; amounts undisclosed publicly. Team size not reliably findable (Crunchbase/PitchBook gated). Other historical backers: SOSV, ZhenFund, Shenzhen Venture Capital, Orient Securities.
business_model: Hardware sales: designs and manufactures robotic arms, cobots, humanoids (Mercury series), and data-collection devices like the myController S570 portable exoskeleton (~$1,300 retail) sold direct via its online store and through distributors (RobotShop, US Robot Store, OpenELAB). Buyers are educators, researchers, and embodied-AI/robotics developers purchasing per-unit hardware rather than subscriptions.
customers: Reports 500+ clients across 51 countries with annual shipments over 50,000 units; named buyer regions include the US, Korea, Japan, and Germany, and the company claims some Fortune 500 customers, but no specific paying org for the S570 line is publicly named (specific named orgs undisclosed).
revenue_status: early-revenue (~$19.4M revenue, third-party estimate via Growjo, unverified)
technical_differentiation: Differentiates in the Layer 3 teleoperation/data-capture niche by offering a low-cost (~$1,300), portable bimanual exoskeleton that captures 100Hz motion data with 12 high-precision (4096-resolution) sensors and integrates directly with its own affordable arm/humanoid ecosystem and open-source LeRobot LfD pipeline. Versus higher-priced Western data-collection rigs from competitors like ALOHA/Trossen Robotics and Galaxea/AgileX teleop kits, its edge is price and a vertically integrated hardware stack rather than proprietary algorithms.

one_liner: AI-native data development platform for managing, curating, labeling, and evaluating multimodal sensor data (video, LiDAR, telemetry) to train robot, drone, and AV perception and policy models.
notes: Founded 2020 by Ulrik Stig Hansen and Eric Landau (YC alum); dual HQ London (origin) and San Francisco. Total $110M across 5 rounds. Latest: Series C $60M led by Wellington Management (Feb 2026), ~$550M valuation; prior Series B $30M led by Next47 (Aug 2024). Platform handles images/video/audio/text/DICOM/LiDAR/telemetry; serves AV, robotics, and drone makers, positioned as a Scale AI rival in physical-AI data infra. Data infra (L3) is arguably the more precise primary layer than Simulation; included as secondary.
business_model: B2B enterprise SaaS: sells a multimodal AI data development platform (three products - Index for data curation/discovery, Annotate for labeling images/video/DICOM/LiDAR, and Active for evaluation) via annual custom-priced subscriptions with sales-led Team and Enterprise tiers (free Starter tier capped at 5 users). Buyers are ML/AV/robotics and medical-AI engineering teams; pricing is custom (not per-seat/per-annotation published), scaling with data volume, products, and company size.
customers: 300+ teams; named customers include Woven by Toyota, Skydio, Zipline, Maxar, AXA Financial, plus medical/research users Stanford Medicine, King's College London, and Memorial Sloan Kettering Cancer Center.
revenue_status: scaling (~$12.8M ARR reported for 2024 via Latka, unverified; company reported revenue grew 4x YoY as of Aug 2024 and physical-AI revenue grew 10x in 12 months as of the Dec 2025 Series C; near cash-flow positive). Total funding $110M across rounds, latest a $60M Series C led by Wellington Management.
technical_differentiation: Positions as an integrated "data development" platform unifying curation (Index), labeling (Annotate), and model evaluation (Active) with a focus on multimodal/physical-AI sensor data (video, LiDAR, telemetry, DICOM), versus Scale AI (services/workforce-heavy labeling) and Labelbox (labeling-centric tooling). Differentiation is the closed-loop curate-label-evaluate workflow and physical-AI/medical multimodal depth rather than pure annotation throughput.

one_liner: XR/VR teleoperation platform (works with Meta Quest, Vive on third-party arms) letting operators remotely manipulate robots while capturing demonstration data in a self-improving loop for robot learning.
notes: London-based (some sources list Reading, UK, home of Henley Business Angels). Founded 2019 by Chang Liu. AMAS allows operators worldwide to control dexterous robots via VR headset (Meta Quest, HTC Vive) on third-party robot arms while capturing synchronized sensor/video/motion data for ML training. Total funding ~$4.26M (PitchBook); investors include Henley Business Angels, Grow London, Connected Places/Digital Catapult, Innovate UK, UK Space Agency, Britbots, Anglia Capital, High Growth Robotics. Latest round: ~£345k via Henley Business Angels (Nov 2025) plus a ~£500k Digital Catapult convertible loan; an earlier formal seed was Nov 2022. Round size/date for Nov 2025 are approximate (~£345k ≈ $430k). In scope: teleoperation/demonstration-data service for robot learning is AI-native. Sources: PitchBook (pitchbook.com/profiles/company/431196-49), Tracxn, Dealroom, Crunchbase, Henley Business Angels.
business_model: Sells its AMAS (Advanced Mechanics Assistance System) XR platform as a cloud SaaS subscription: operators use Meta Quest/Vive headsets to teleoperate third-party robot arms and humanoids while capturing manipulation demonstration data for AI training. Buyers are industrial enterprises (nuclear decommissioning, manufacturing, agriculture, logistics) and humanoid robot deployers; pricing is quote/contract-based and not publicly disclosed. Also acts as an official integrator for Unitree humanoids and supports paid pilots/grant-funded projects (Innovate UK, UK Space Agency, EU Vojext).
customers: Named partners and pilot deployments include Leyland Trucks and Paccar Australia (truck-assembly automation trials), Saffron Grange Vineyard (grape harvesting), UK pharmacy/care-sector demos, plus integration partners Unitree, NVIDIA, SenseGlove and the Agri-OpenCore consortium and Open University 'Gatekeeper' project. Most appear to be trials/grant-funded projects rather than confirmed large paying contracts; specific paying customers are largely undisclosed.
revenue_status: early-revenue
technical_differentiation: Differentiates on consumer-XR (Quest/Vive) immersive teleoperation that is hardware-agnostic across third-party arms and humanoids while simultaneously capturing demonstration data for imitation learning, versus teleoperation/data-collection peers like Kinova/AtkinsRealis (nuclear-specific, proprietary arms) and humanoid-data players such as the Boston Dynamics Spot/RAICo Sellafield programs. Positioning as a Unitree integrator and NVIDIA collaborator targets the humanoid demonstration-data niche, though it competes against well-capitalized teleop-data entrants.

also_known_as: Foxglove Technologies, Inc.; formerly Foxglove Studio
one_liner: Multimodal data and observability platform letting roboticists record, visualize, and manage sensor/training data (MCAP format) to accelerate training and deployment of physical-AI robots.
notes: Founded 2021 by ex-Cruise engineers Adrian Macneil and Roman Shtylman (built similar internal AV tooling at Cruise). HQ San Francisco, CA. Funding history: Seed (Amplify), $15M Series A Oct 2022 led by Eclipse Ventures (angels incl. Kyle Vogt, Pieter Abbeel), $40M Series B Nov 2025 led by Bessemer Venture Partners with Eclipse and Amplify participating; total $58M+. Team ~50 (half engineers). Product: visualization/recording tooling and the open-source MCAP container format adopted by ROS 2 and NVIDIA Isaac, plus LeRobot integration and teleoperation stream visualization. In_scope=true: robotics-data-native (demonstration/training-data + sim/observability for robot learning); core category exists specifically to feed learned robot policies. Borderline as enhancement per prompt, but classified in-scope under default rule. Sources: The Robot Report, Business Wire, VentureBeat, Crunchbase, foxglove.dev/about.
business_model: Hybrid usage-based SaaS plus enterprise contracts for its multimodal robotics data/observability platform: a free tier, a Pro plan at $20/month + ~$42/developer seat, ~$20/device, and metered storage/query/bandwidth overages, and custom Enterprise contracts (dedicated support, air-gapped/on-prem deploys, forward-deployed engineers). Buyers are robotics/Physical AI engineering teams; the open MCAP logging format is free and drives adoption funneling into the paid cloud platform.
customers: Named users/customers include NVIDIA, Amazon, Anduril, Wayve, and Dexterity (Dexterity reports ~$150K/year and 20%+ dev-time savings); platform cited as used by tens of thousands of developers.
revenue_status: scaling (no public revenue/ARR figure disclosed; raised $40M Series B Nov 2025 led by Bessemer, >$58M total since 2021)
technical_differentiation: Differentiates by authoring and stewarding the open MCAP multimodal logging standard (bundled by default in ROS 2 and NVIDIA Isaac), paired with a petabyte-scale data lake plus best-in-class robotics visualization, versus rerun.io (open-source multimodal visualizer, weaker managed data-platform/storage layer) and Roboto AI (robotics data management/observability but without an entrenched logging-format standard).

also_known_as: Foxtech, FOXTECH ROBOT, Foxtech UAV; legal entity Huixinghai Technology (Tianjin) Co., Ltd
one_liner: Sells ExoArm-7 wearable 7-DOF/arm exoskeleton (<20ms latency) for teleoperating robotic arms and capturing human-demonstration datasets for VLA and imitation/reinforcement learning, OpenArm-compatible.
notes: Foxtech is a Tianjin, China UAV/drone manufacturer founded 2010 by Jack Fu (drone-market entry, origins ~2007) operating across 55 countries. In 2025 it launched a robotics division (Foxtech Robotics / FOXTECH ROBOT) with embodied-AI products including the ExoArm-7 teleoperation exoskeleton, Agility A1/A2 OpenArm-based research arms, and dual-arm bimanual teleop kits. ExoArm-7: 14 DOF (7/arm), 14-bit absolute encoders, <20ms latency, ROS2/NVIDIA Isaac support, OpenArm-compatible, SDK included; explicitly marketed for collecting human-demonstration data for VLA, imitation and reinforcement learning. Classified L3 (data infrastructure / teleop-demonstration-data hardware) with secondary L4 hardware. In-scope: teleoperation/demonstration-data tooling is AI-native robot-learning infrastructure. Legal entity appears to be Huixinghai Technology (Tianjin) Co., Ltd; private company, funding undisclosed (no Crunchbase/VC rounds found). Marked as division because robotics is a 2025 sub-line of the larger Foxtech drone business. Founding year refers to parent Foxtech (2010); robotics line dates to 2025.
business_model: Direct hardware sales of robotics teleoperation gear through its online store (store.foxtech.com): the ExoArm-7 wearable exoskeleton controller is listed at $3,580 USD per unit, sold to robotics/AI researchers as a leader device for teleoperating arms and capturing human-demonstration data. Foxtech upsells companion hardware (OpenArm-based Agility A1/A2 follower arms and dual-arm platforms) plus an SDK, so revenue is per-unit and transactional with no recurring/SaaS component; the broader company also sells industrial UAVs/drones and UGVs.
customers: Undisclosed at the named-org level. Marketed to and purchased by robotics/embodied-AI researchers, AI developers, and academic institutions (research labs, universities, robotics courses) for imitation-learning/VLA dataset collection; no specific paying or piloting organizations are named in public sources.
revenue_status: undisclosed (no public revenue or ARR figure for the robotics line; parent Foxtech is a privately held Tianjin, China UAV/drone maker operating since 2010 with no disclosed financials)
technical_differentiation: Differentiates on a 7-DOF (14-DOF dual-arm) wearable exoskeleton leader with <20ms latency and 14-bit absolute encoders, sold as a low-cost, open-source-compatible (OpenArm/ROS2/NVIDIA Isaac) teleoperation+data-capture bundle. Versus Stanford's open-source ALOHA/Mobile ALOHA puppeteering rigs and AgileX/Galaxea teleoperation kits, its edge is a commercially packaged ergonomic exoskeleton form factor with SDK rather than a leader-follower joint-copy arm; otherwise largely undifferentiated, competing on price, packaging, and open-hardware compatibility.

one_liner: Crowdsources real-world robot teleoperation data via gamified low-cost sidewalk rovers ('Drive to Earn'), releasing open datasets and building BitRobot, a crypto-incentivized subnet network for embodied-AI research.
notes: L3 Data Infrastructure: demonstration-data network for robot learning. Released open FrodoBots-2K dataset (~2,000 hrs teleoperated sidewalk-robot data — camera, GPS, IMU, audio, human control — across 10+ cities). Co-developed BitRobot with Protocol Labs (Juan Benet), a Solana-based 'Bittensor for robotics' network of subnets to scale embodied-AI data collection across embodiments. Feb 2025 round was $6M seed led by Protocol VC (participation: Big Brain Holdings, Fabric Ventures, Zee Prime, Tioga, Sfermion, Solana Ventures, Virtuals Protocol; angels incl. Solana's Anatoly Yakovenko, Raj Gokal), bringing total to $8M. Cofounder: Michael Cho. Founded ~2022. Earth Rovers are low-cost ($250+) sidewalk robots; Earth Rover Challenge run with DeepMind researchers. Secondary L4 because it designs the rover hardware, but primary value is the teleoperation/demonstration-data network. in_scope=true: AI-native demonstration-data service for robot learning, would not exist without learned embodied policies. Team size not publicly disclosed.
business_model: Two revenue layers: (1) sells low-cost teleoperable sidewalk robots (Earth Rovers, and Octo Arms) to consumers/gamers at ~$149-$299 per unit, where each device doubles as a data-collection node; (2) monetizes the resulting real-world teleoperation data (video/audio/control/GPS, e.g. the FrodoBots-2K dataset) via licensing/API access, and is building BitRobot, a Solana-based crypto-incentivized DePIN network of subnets that rewards robot owners and data contributors ('Drive to Earn') to scale embodied-AI training data. Token/network economics (BitRobot) are intended to be the primary long-term monetization, not the thin-margin hardware sales.
customers: No disclosed paying customers; data buyers are not publicly named. Research/usage partners include Google DeepMind and UC Berkeley (Earth Rover Challenge collaborators); the FrodoBots-2K open dataset has reportedly been used by teams at DeepMind, Meta, and UC Berkeley. Ecosystem partner: Yield Guild Games (YGG). These are collaborators/users, not confirmed paying customers - paying customers undisclosed.
revenue_status: pre-revenue (no public revenue/ARR figure; funded by ~$8M total raised - $2M pre-seed plus $6M seed led by Protocol VC, Feb 2025)
technical_differentiation: Differentiates on radically low-cost gamified data collection: ~$200 bill-of-materials sidewalk rovers (vs ~$5,500 for Starship Technologies) crowdsourced via 'Drive to Earn' gaming, yielding the largest public urban robot-navigation dataset (FrodoBots-2K, ~2,000 hrs). Vs DePIN/crypto-robotics peers it positions BitRobot as a 'Bittensor for Robotics' (multi-subnet, co-developed with Protocol Labs), distinguishing it from single-embodiment plays and from pure teleoperation-data labelers that lack a deployed consumer hardware fleet and token-incentivized network.

one_liner: Builds embodied-AI robot training data: full-scenario real-robot collection plus a heterogeneous platform compatible with mainstream robot protocols and AI-driven annotation for rapid multi-body onboarding.
last_round_size_usd: undisclosed (tens of millions RMB; ~USD low tens of millions)
notes: Hangzhou-based AI data company (est. 2012-04-24, Xiaoshan District; legal rep Zheng Xiaowei). Core business is general data collection/annotation (image, audio, text, video, 3D point cloud, multimodal) via self-developed SolarSense corpus platform; clients include Huawei, Alibaba, Hikvision. Qualifies for census via its dedicated embodied-intelligence robotics data product (ei.html): full-scenario real-robot (真机) data collection across home/hotel/retail/office/industrial scenes, multimodal capture (video, motion trajectory, pose, tactile), heterogeneous embodied-data platform compatible with mainstream robot comms protocols for rapid multi-body onboarding, AI-driven annotation, and ability to stand up 1000-person standardized collection teams. Two regional collection centers in Chongqing (Liangjiang New Area) and Guiyang (>10,000 sqm, 700+ stations per assignment brief). In-scope: teleoperation/demonstration data service for robot learning (AI-native), not general labeling. Pre-A round May 2025: "tens of millions" RMB, exact size undisclosed; for public-data operations and vertical annotation bases. Total funding and headcount not publicly disclosed. Robotics data is a division/product line, not the whole company.
business_model: Sells AI training-data services on a project/contract basis: scene setup, real-robot ("真机") multimodal data collection (video, motion trajectory, posture, tactile), annotation, QC, and custom dataset production via its intelligent corpus engineering platform, plus pre-built structured datasets and a heterogeneous collection platform compatible with mainstream robot protocols. Buyers are large-model developers, robotics/embodied-AI firms, AI research institutions and government public-data programs, billed per bespoke data project/dataset (specific pricing undisclosed).
customers: Reported to serve mainstream large-model vendors Alibaba, Huawei and Tencent, plus embodied-AI/autonomous-driving firms DJI and Great Wall Motor; customer base also spans government bodies and AI research institutions (1000+ customers claimed). Embodied-AI-specific named buyers are not separately disclosed.
revenue_status: early-revenue (no absolute figure disclosed; company states 2025 projected revenue growth of 400%+ YoY, unverified; closed tens-of-millions RMB Pre-A round led by a Hangzhou Jintou Group fund, May 2025)
technical_differentiation: Differentiates via a heterogeneous real-robot collection platform compatible with mainstream robot protocols spanning five physical scenarios (home, hotel, retail, office, industrial) with timestamp-aligned multimodal capture, plus a 27-province/52-country collection network and standardized equipment ops, versus pure-play data-labeling rivals like Stardust (星尘数据) and Appen-style firms that lean more on simulation or generic annotation. Edge is execution/scale and physical collection infrastructure rather than a unique proprietary algorithm.

also_known_as: HaptX Inc.; formerly AxonVR; now a 1HMX brand
one_liner: Makes microfluidic haptic gloves delivering realistic tactile and force feedback plus sub-millimeter motion tracking, used as teleoperation rigs to collect high-fidelity dexterous manipulation demonstration data for robot training.
notes: Borderline scope case. HaptX (founded 2012 as AxonVR) is fundamentally a microfluidic haptic-glove hardware company that predates the robot-learning wave; gloves were originally a VR product. Increasingly positioned as a manipulation-demonstration teleop interface: HaptX Gloves G1 used by Sanctuary AI for "teach by feel" dexterous manipulation, and with Shadow Robot on the ANA-funded tactile telerobot. Marketed for imitation-learning data collection (sub-millimeter tracking, tactile/pressure data). In 2024 HaptX combined with manufacturing partner Advanced Input Systems and the entity adopted the name 1HMX; HaptX is now a 1HMX brand. 1HMX launched the Nexus NX1 turnkey teleoperation/data-capture rig (HaptX gloves + Virtuix Omni One treadmill + Freeaim shoes) in Nov 2025, pre-order with Q2 2026 shipping. Funding figures ($61.45M total, last disclosed $12M round July 2021) predate the 1HMX merger; current entity-level funding undisclosed. Assigned L3 Data Infrastructure but the product is primarily L4 hardware serving demonstration-data collection (L6). The hardware itself contains no learned policy; AI is downstream in customers' imitation-learning pipelines. Sources: Crunchbase, GeekWire (2021-07), haptx.com, Humanoids Daily / The Robot Report (2025).
business_model: Sells microfluidic haptic hardware - flagship HaptX Gloves G1 - to enterprise/government buyers for VR training, design, and robot teleoperation, priced as low as ~$4,500 per pair (up to ~$5,495 single pair) plus a mandatory recurring HaptX Subscription starting at $495/month for service, software (incl. ROS-node SDK for telerobotics), and maintenance. Revenue is hardware + subscription, sold via direct enterprise pre-orders and now bundled into the 1HMX/Nexus NX1 whole-body teleoperation rig.
customers: "Dozens of Fortune 500 companies and government agencies" deployed early Development Kits. Named: Shadow Robot and All Nippon Airways (ANA) consortium for the Tactile Telerobot teleoperation system; Verizon (also a strategic investor via Verizon Ventures). Nissan reported as a customer (unverified). Most named end-buyers remain undisclosed.
revenue_status: early-revenue (no audited figure; company cites "tens of millions of dollars" in G1 pre-orders and a "multi-million-dollar customer response," unverified; ~$61M total raised through Series B)
technical_differentiation: Proprietary microfluidic skin delivering true displacement-based tactile feedback (hundreds of actuators per hand) plus force feedback and sub-millimeter tracking - more physically realistic than vibrotactile/tendon competitors like bHaptics and SenseGlove (Nova/DK1), and unlike Shadow Robot (a partner/integrator) HaptX supplies the human-side haptic interface itself.

also_known_as: 整数智能, MolarData, ZhengShu ZhiNeng, 整数智能信息技术（杭州）有限责任公司, MooreData
one_liner: AI data partner offering the MolarData/MooreData engineering platform and ACE dataset-construction service, including embodied-AI VLA and robot-operation multimodal datasets with AI-assisted labeling.
total_funding_usd: undisclosed (tens of millions RMB across Pre-A and A rounds; ~USD low tens of millions)
last_round_size_usd: undisclosed (数千万元 RMB, ~USD several million)
notes: Spun out of Zhejiang University Institute of Computer Innovation Technology (2019), HQ Hangzhou. Provides MolarData/MooreData intelligent data-engineering platform + ACE dataset-construction service (collection/cleaning/annotation) across image/video/text/audio/point-cloud. Explicit embodied-AI (具身智能) line building VLA multimodal and robot-operation datasets with AI-assisted labeling and MLOps loop; serves 1,000-2,000+ tech/research orgs incl. embodied-AI teams. Listed on Zhejiang Seed Unicorn TOP100 (AI & Embodied Intelligence). Funding: Pre-A (2023-06, tens of millions RMB, investors incl. Yizhou Capital, Oufang Angel, Turing Qizhen) and Series A (2024-08, tens of millions RMB, led by Fengrui Capital) to fund dataset-construction service, platform, and global expansion. Exact USD amounts and team size not publicly disclosed. in_scope=true: dedicated robotics/embodied-AI data product (teleoperation/demonstration-style VLA datasets), not AV-only. Sources: molardata.com; 36kr.com/p/2902815664806528 (Series A); chuangtouzhijia.com Pre-A; gelonghui.com (Fengrui-led A); qcc.com company registry. Secondary L6 tag reflects robotics/embodied-AI vertical focus of the data product; primary remains L3 Data Infrastructure.
business_model: B2B AI data services: sells data collection, cleaning, and annotation (the ACE dataset-construction service) plus the MolarData/MooreData intelligent data-engineering platform with AI-assisted labeling, billed as project-based data delivery contracts and platform/tooling licensing to model builders in autonomous driving, generative AI, and embodied AI (including VLA/robot-operation datasets). Specific per-unit/seat pricing is not publicly disclosed.
customers: Claims cooperation with 1,000+ top tech companies and research institutions across intelligent driving, genAI, and embodied AI; one named partnership is with Shanghai Kupasi Technology (库帕思科技) on a corpus/data ecosystem. Specific paying-customer names are otherwise undisclosed.
revenue_status: early-revenue (~$9.5M revenue/ARR 2025, unverified third-party Getlatka; valuation ~$28.4M, ~86 employees)
technical_differentiation: Differentiates from global leaders Scale AI and Appen via a China/APAC-rooted, lower-cost engineering platform (MolarData/MooreData) with heavy AI-assisted 4D annotation claiming 500-1000% efficiency gains and early specialization in embodied-AI VLA/robot-operation datasets; versus domestic rivals like Testin Cloud (云测数据) and Datatang it leans on its Zhejiang University origin and an integrated MLOps closed-loop platform plus global offices (Silicon Valley, Paris, Singapore, Tokyo).

company_name: Lingyu Intelligence (北京灵御智能科技有限公司)
one_liner: Tackles embodied-AI's data bottleneck via low-cost, high-precision teleoperation robots (TeleAvatar) that collect real-world manipulation demonstrations to train dexterous robot policies.
last_round_lead: Galaxy Innovation Capital (银河创新资本); earlier seed+ led by Huaying Capital / Meridian (华映资本)
notes: Tsinghua Automation Dept spin-out, founded Feb 2025. Co-founders: Mo Yilin (莫一林, tenured associate professor at Tsinghua Automation, CMU PhD, Caltech postdoc, chief scientist) and Jin Ge (金戈, CEO, ex-Telescope Ventures managing partner). Positions as "embodied-AI infrastructure provider" — human-side teleoperation data collection + machine-side dexterous execution + intelligent operation platform. Flagship product TeleAvatar: low-cost (~10k RMB range), low-latency, high-precision, ready-to-use teleoperation robot. Strategy: "sell robot body → sell data → sell capabilities," targeting fully autonomous robots over time. Cumulative funding ~100M RMB across seed, seed+ (Huaying/Meridian Capital lead; Inno Angel, Shuimu Tsinghua alumni seed fund, Telescope VC), and a ~100M-aggregate angel/angel+ round (announced March 2026, led by Galaxy Innovation Capital, with Lihechang Ventures and Jinshajiang Joint Capital). PitchBook shows only $1.37M disclosed (incomplete vs RMB-reported aggregate). First commercial deliveries reported Sept 2025. In-scope: AI-native teleoperation/demonstration-data and embodied data-infra company — the category exists to feed robot learning. Primary layer L3 Data Infrastructure; secondary L4 (teleoperation hardware) and L6 (data-collection robots). Exact most-recent round size not publicly itemized.
business_model: Direct hardware sales of its TeleAvatar teleoperation robot system (machine-side robot, CyberBraceletVR human-side wearable, and TeleDroid control platform) to robotics/embodied-AI labs and companies that need to collect real-world manipulation demonstration data. Sold per-unit at a deliberately low price (wheeled dual-arm "TA lite" from ~79,900 RMB / ~$11K), with future upside in selling the collected demonstration datasets / data-as-a-service for training manipulation policies.
customers: First-batch named delivery customers include the Xigang-Shanghai Robot Dexterous Intelligence Research Institute (received unit 001) and Shanghai Jiyu Intelligent Technology Co., Ltd.; "over ten units" delivered to additional unnamed strategic-cooperation customers in Sept 2025.
revenue_status: early-revenue (no public revenue/ARR figure disclosed; first commercial deliveries began Sept 2025 at ~79,900 RMB/unit starting price)
technical_differentiation: Competes on extreme cost-precision tradeoff in teleoperation data collection: ~100ms image latency / ~50ms operation latency and a sub-100K RMB price (claimed lowest mass-produced wheeled humanoid), undercutting higher-priced teleoperation rigs from data-layer peers such as ARX/AgileX (松灵/AgileX teleoperation arms) and the dexterous-hand-based capture rigs from 灵心巧手 (LinkerHand). Differentiation is the "human-machine hybrid intelligence" wearable (CyberBraceletVR) plus a Tsinghua Automation motion-control team, rather than a proprietary moat, so partly competing on execution/capital.

one_liner: Two-sided marketplace mobilizing 3M+ vetted global contributors to collect rights-cleared egocentric/multimodal demonstration video, delivering spec-built datasets for robotics perception and embodied-AI policy training.
last_round_lead: General Catalyst and Lightspeed Venture Partners (co-led)
notes: YC W26 batch. Founders William Namgyal (CEO) and Inigo Lenderking (COO). Rights-cleared, audit-ready multimodal data marketplace + collection engine; global contributor network across ~100 countries (cited as 3M+ vetted contributors). Robotics-relevant work includes egocentric demonstration video (cooking, household chores, warehouse operations, gemstone carving) plus curated Ego4D/Ego-Exo4D subsets used for embodied-AI/robot policy training. Borderline scope: human first-person/egocentric video rather than robot teleop rigs, but included per the census's robotics-demonstration-data framing — in_scope=true. Round co-led by General Catalyst and Lightspeed (~$31.2M seed); backed by YC plus angels from xAI, Meta, DoorDash, Apple. Round date approximate (~early 2026, W26 launch period). Funding source: https://www.luel.ai/resources/blog/luel-seed-31m-general-catalyst-lightspeed and Lightspeed announcement (lsvp.com). HQ and founding-year source: YC company page.
business_model: Two-sided marketplace: enterprise AI/robotics buyers commission custom rights-cleared multimodal datasets (egocentric/manipulation video with aligned sensor/pose "physics layer," plus speech/audio), which Luel sources from a vetted global contributor network, QAs, and delivers audit-ready within days. Pricing varies by project scope (flat fee, per-minute, or revenue-share), and completed datasets are re-licensed to other customers to build a recurring catalog.
customers: Stated buyer base spans generative AI labs, robotics/embodied-AI companies, speech research teams, major social platforms, universities, hospitals, and banks (40+ active campaigns); no specific named paying customers are publicly disclosed.
revenue_status: early-revenue (no public revenue/ARR figure; raised $31.2M seed led by Lightspeed with General Catalyst and SV Angel, one of the largest seed rounds in YC history)
technical_differentiation: Vs. Kled (direct YC-era rival, a mobile/Solana-blockchain consumer data app skewed to photos and token payouts), Luel positions as an enterprise procurement layer emphasizing spec'd campaigns, multi-stage QA, rights/provenance clearance, and embodied-AI data where clips ship with a physics layer (frame-aligned sensor streams, device pose, hand-object interaction). Vs. Scale AI/Surge AI (annotation/RLHF incumbents), differentiates on collection of novel egocentric/manipulation data rather than labeling, competing on speed and legal clarity over price-per-gigabyte.

also_known_as: 鹿明机器人; Lumos Robotics; 鹿明机器人科技（深圳）有限公司
one_liner: Builds FastUMI portable handheld/backpack rigs capturing 1-3mm-precision real-robot manipulation demonstrations at ~3-5x efficiency and 1/5 cost to train embodied-AI manipulation policies.
last_round_lead: Shenneng Chengyi (Pre-A2); CDH Investments / 鼎晖 led Pre-A1
notes: Registered as 鹿明机器人科技（深圳）有限公司 on 2024-09-06 in Shenzhen, CN (team ties to Tsinghua/SJTU/CUHK; some coverage references Shanghai). Founder/CEO Yu Chao (喻超), ex-head of Dreame humanoid-robot business, Tsinghua, robot-learning since 2016, contributed to Xiaomi CyberDog; CTO Cao Junliang (SJTU PhD); co-CTO Ding Yan (丁焱), former Shanghai AI Lab researcher who brought UMI from lab to industry. Funding: completed angel, angel++ (Fosun RZ Capital 复星锐正, Demai/德马科技, Wuzhong Financial Holding), Pre-A1 (led by CDH/鼎晖; Nanjing Chuangtou, Jinjing Capital, Jingu shares followed), and Pre-A2 (Shenneng Chengyi/申能诚毅) — totaling nearly 200M RMB (~USD 28M est). Strategic shareholders incl. Fosun Group and SenseTime/商汤. FastUMI Pro: multimodal body-free data-collection HW/SW, world-first backpack UMI rig; 8-step industrial QA raising data validity from ~70% to 95%+; data 'supermarket' covering 10 scenarios/40+ tasks. 2026 plan: deploy 10,000 backpack units across 6 real-world scenarios (industrial, home, hotel, restaurant, mall, office) and build 1M+ hours of real-robot embodied data capacity. Partner: Mitsubishi Electric. In-scope: AI-native teleoperation/demonstration data-collection hardware+service for learned manipulation. total_funding and team_size are estimates/unknown; round USD sizes undisclosed (RMB only). Sources: pandaily.com; kr-asia.com; finance.sina.com.cn; stcn.com; chnfund.com; baike.baidu.com.
business_model: Sells embodied-AI manipulation data and the hardware to produce it: the FastUMI Pro portable/backpack UMI data-collection rigs (sold/deployed to robotics teams) plus a "FastUMI Pro Data Supermarket" (鹿明数据超市) marketplace offering real-robot demonstration datasets covering 10 scenarios and 40+ tasks. Revenue comes from selling rigs and per-hour/dataset real-robot manipulation data to AI model builders, plus scenario deployment contracts; targeting 1M+ hours/year data-collection capacity in 2026.
customers: Named: Mitsubishi (三菱) and China COSCO Shipping (中远海运), per 36Kr; company also claims dozens of domestic/overseas data customers including "over two-thirds of top global embodied-AI teams" and leading university labs (specific lab names undisclosed).
revenue_status: early-revenue (no public revenue/ARR figure disclosed; founded Sept 2024, has paying data customers and head-customer orders e.g. Mitsubishi; raised several-hundred-million-yuan Pre-A1/Pre-A2 rounds)
technical_differentiation: Positions on a portable/backpack-form FastUMI Pro rig that cuts single-demo capture from ~50s to ~10s (5x efficiency, ~1/5 cost, 1-3mm precision, data-validity raised from ~70% to >95%) versus the academic open-source UMI (Stanford/Columbia handheld gripper) baseline it extends; differentiated on capture throughput and a packaged data-marketplace versus Chinese data peers and in-house teleoperation collection.

one_liner: AI training-data vendor offering a robotics line: custom manipulation-trajectory collection, multi-sensor teleop pipelines, egocentric POV data, and VLA training data with robotics QA.
notes: Broader AI training-data/annotation vendor (founded 2022, Noida, India; bootstrapped/unfunded per Tracxn/Crunchbase; ~$8.6M 2025 revenue, ~74 staff per getLatka/Tracxn) with a dedicated robotics/embodied-AI offering: custom hardware-specific manipulation-trajectory collection, multi-sensor teleoperation with sensor sync, egocentric POV data, VLA training data, tactile sensing data, and robotics-specific QA. Layer 3 Data Infrastructure as a division of a general data company. in_scope=true: teleoperation/demonstration data services for robot learning are AI-native. Founder: Harshul Arora.
business_model: Macgence sells custom AI training-data and data-annotation services on a project/contract basis to AI and robotics developers, including a robotics line (manipulation-trajectory collection, multi-sensor teleop pipelines, egocentric POV video, and VLA-model datasets) plus multilingual annotation/RLHF; revenue comes from per-project and managed data-services engagements rather than a SaaS subscription. Bootstrapped (no external VC), pricing is bespoke per-dataset/per-annotation-volume.
customers: No named robotics customers are publicly disclosed; Clutch/case-study references describe unnamed clients (an "AI development company" chatbot project across five languages, an "AI-powered telecom solutions" company, and a "major cloud-based voice service provider" for 40+ language assistant training). Customer base described as enterprises, startups, universities, and individual researchers; specific paying robotics orgs are undisclosed.
revenue_status: early-revenue (~$8.6M revenue 2025, per Getlatka; 78-person team, bootstrapped — unverified third-party estimate)
technical_differentiation: Differentiates via breadth of human-in-the-loop multilingual data (300+ languages) combined with a dedicated embodied-AI/robotics line (teleop trajectory + egocentric + VLA datasets), versus larger horizontal data-labeling vendors like Scale AI and Appen; competes largely on lower-cost India-based delivery and execution rather than proprietary technology, so positioning is closer to "undifferentiated - competing on execution/capital" against well-funded incumbents.

also_known_as: Maniformer (Shanghai); AgiBot data unit; 觅蜂科技 (Mifeng Technology)
one_liner: Embodied-AI data infrastructure platform providing real-robot, simulation, and human-demonstration data plus MEgo robot-free capture hardware to overcome the robot-learning training-data bottleneck.
notes: AgiBot-incubated (智元机器人) embodied-AI data infrastructure unit, founded Feb 2026 in Shanghai (Pudong, Xiupu Road). Distinct legal entity from AgiBot itself. Chinese name 觅蜂科技 (Mifeng Technology). Provides full-spectrum data: real-robot, simulation, and human-demonstration data, with MEgo series hardware (grippers, head-mounted devices) enabling robot-free human demonstration capture of motion/vision/tactile. Raised hundreds of millions RMB seed/angel led by HongShan/HSG (rebranded Sequoia China) with participation from DH VGC (Dinghui/Dinghuai), Baidu Ventures, Yunfeng Capital, Muhua Science Innovation, plus strategic investors. Some sources headline "Sequoia China" and one (Preqin/36kr) references HSG/ByteDance in larger embodied deals; HSG and Sequoia China are the same firm. In scope: AI-native — teleoperation/demonstration data services and sim-to-real data infra for robot learning ("Scale AI for robotics"); targets tens of millions of data-hours vs ~500k high-quality hours globally. Exact USD funding, total funding, and team size not publicly disclosed. Primary layer L3 Data Infrastructure; secondary L4 (MEgo hardware) and L2 (simulation data). [Merged duplicate: 觅蜂科技 (Maniformer)] Embodied-AI data platform incubated by AgiBot providing full-chain real-robot, teleoperation, simulation and tactile training data plus a marketplace of embodied datasets for robot learning.
business_model: Sells embodied-AI training data and tooling: (1) curated real-robot, simulation, and human-demonstration datasets via a data marketplace/"Hive" co-creation program; (2) MEgo data-collection hardware (grippers, head-mounted/wearable capture rigs enabling robot-free demonstration capture); and (3) end-to-end data services (collection, governance, simulation assets). Buyers are robot/foundation-model developers and research institutions; sold via direct enterprise contracts and per-dataset/marketplace purchases (specific pricing not publicly disclosed).
customers: Maniformer states it serves "leading global enterprises and research institutions" but discloses no named clients. As the data unit incubated by AgiBot (Zhiyuan Robotics), AgiBot itself is the natural anchor consumer of its data. Strategic investors PsiBot/PSI (灵初智能) and Junpu Intelligent are industry partners. Named paying/piloting customers otherwise undisclosed.
revenue_status: early-revenue (no public revenue/ARR figure disclosed; founded Feb 2026, raised several hundred million RMB seed+angel led by Sequoia China)
technical_differentiation: Differentiates on MEgo robot-free / human-wearable data capture (grippers + head-mounted sensors capturing motion, vision and tactile data without a physical robot), plus a cross-embodiment data-governance engine to standardize data across robot morphologies, and AgiBot's first-party robot fleet for real-machine data. Versus teleoperation/robot-farm data approaches used by peers like AgiBot's own collection ops and data houses serving Galbot/Unitree ecosystems, the robot-free capture aims to scale collection volume (tens of millions of hours target) far more cheaply than robot-in-the-loop teleoperation.

also_known_as: Manus Meta, Manus VR, Manus-Meta, MANUS Technology Group
one_liner: High-precision EMF data gloves capturing 25-DOF hand articulation at mm accuracy to record human demonstration and teleoperation data for training robots and VLA models.
notes: Device vendor (data gloves) whose output is robot demonstration/teleoperation training data; in_scope=true because value is demonstration-data capture for learned policies (Metagloves Pro / Pro Haptic, EMF tracking, 25-DOF, mm precision). Official data glove of NVIDIA Isaac Teleop; explicit 'Data Gloves for Teleoperation & Humanoid Robot Training' line; serves 2000+ robotics/AI orgs. HQ Geldrop, NL (per about-us page; De Hooge Akker 22); main office Eindhoven plus offices in Germany and US. CAUTION: do NOT confuse with 'Manus AI' (Chinese AI-agent startup, Series B $75M Benchmark/Tencent, Apr 2025) — a different company; that funding is NOT this entity. This data-glove company's only confirmed funding is early ~EUR2M Series A (Eindhoven Venture Capital Fund II) / ~$3.48M total (investors ECFG, High Tech XL, Startupbootcamp) per CB Insights/Crunchbase/Bits&Chips; later rounds undisclosed. Founder/CEO Stephan van den Brink. [Merged duplicate: MANUS (Manus Meta)] Makes high-precision data gloves and full-body mocap (Metagloves Pro, Quantum Metagloves, Polygon) that capture finger-level human demonstration data for teleoperation and humanoid-robot training.
business_model: Sells precision EMF data gloves as hardware with bundled software (MANUS Core SDK with C++/ROS2 support); flagship products are Metagloves Pro and Metagloves Pro Haptic, with the prior Quantum Metagloves having sold for ~$9,000/€7,500 per unit including a perpetual license and 2-year warranty. Buyers are robotics/AI labs, automotive, and mocap studios purchasing gloves (plus trackers/SDK) for teleoperation and humanoid robot training-data capture.
customers: Company markets being "trusted by 2000+" robotics/AI/XR/mocap organizations; named enterprise customers cited include BMW, Audi, Airbus, Rolls-Royce, Netflix, and Google. (These are largely mocap/XR references; specific robotics-training buyers are not individually disclosed.)
revenue_status: undisclosed (no public revenue/ARR figure; PitchBook lists ~$3.48M total funding raised, ~35 employees, founded 2014, investors HighTechXL/Startupbootcamp/ECFG — note the widely reported "$100M ARR / $2B Meta acquisition" figures belong to the unrelated Manus AI software company, NOT this Dutch hardware firm)
technical_differentiation: Uses EMF (electromagnetic field) finger tracking delivering ~mm accuracy across 25-DOF full anatomical hand models with low latency and no optical occlusion, versus optical/IMU mocap gloves from Rokoko (Smartgloves) and stretch-sensor/haptic gloves from HaptX and Contact CI; differentiation is robotics-grade dexterity capture plus ROS2/C++ teleoperation integration rather than VR/animation-only focus.

one_liner: Robot training-data company capturing large-scale egocentric human-demonstration video via body sensors and iPhones, then labeling it to train humanoid/robot VLA policies.
notes: L3 Data Infrastructure: "the data engine for physical AI" / data layer for robotics. Hires baristas, cooks, mechanics to film basic tasks; captures fine human motion via custom body sensors and iPhones (egocentric video, 3D hand poses, camera motion, task descriptions), then labels raw video at scale to train robot/humanoid VLA policies. Contributor to EgoVerse open egocentric dataset (with Georgia Tech, Stanford, UCSD, ETH Zurich, MIT, Meta Reality Labs, Scale AI; released April 2026). Founders: Josh Gao (CEO), Mogen Cheng, Jason Chong, Duy Nguyen. Funding: ~$8M seed (led by Neo; SV Angel, A-Star Capital, Cade, Timeless, Offline VC) + $60M total across a $25M Series A (closed Nov 2025, led by Framework Ventures) and a $35M follow-on (Menlo Ventures, SV Angel, Kindred Ventures, angel Ted Xiao). Total ~$68M. Projects ~$100M ARR on signed contracts. HQ New York City; founded 2025; ~40 employees. in_scope=true: AI-native teleoperation/demonstration data service for robot learning, would not exist without learned policies. Marked core (pure training-data company); L6_vertical noted as secondary since data feeds vertical robot/humanoid manipulation tasks. mecka.ai returned 403 to automated fetch; details corroborated by Fortune, FinSMEs, upstartsmedia, citybiz.
business_model: Sells robot training datasets and model-integration services: it pays gig workers (baristas, cooks, mechanics) to capture large-scale egocentric human-demonstration video via body sensors and iPhones, then labels it (3D hand poses, camera motion, task descriptions) and sells the corpora/training support to humanoid and embodied-AI developers, primarily via signed enterprise data contracts (a Scale AI-style "picks-and-shovels" model for physical AI). Specific per-dataset/contract pricing is not publicly disclosed.
customers: Home humanoid maker 1X Technologies is named as an early partner; Mecka also claims paid contracts with several Fortune 100 corporations but declines to name them (otherwise undisclosed).
revenue_status: scaling (~$100M projected annual run rate from already-signed contracts, per CEO; described by an investor as the fastest-growing-revenue company they have backed)
technical_differentiation: Differentiates from generalist data-labeling rivals Scale AI and Encord by focusing specifically on robot-free, in-the-wild egocentric human-demonstration capture (body sensors + iPhones yielding 3D hand pose / camera motion data, contributing to the EgoVerse dataset) rather than teleoperation or broad multimodal labeling, and by moving up-stack into model integration/training rather than pure data aggregation.

one_liner: Human data engine vetting domain experts to produce robotics teleoperation trajectories, expert demonstrations, and action labeling for VLA and imitation-learning policy training.
notes: Founded 2022 by Ali Ansari (CEO); HQ Palo Alto, CA. Series A of $35M at $500M valuation led by 01 Advisors (Dick Costolo & Adam Bain) announced Sep 2025 (TechCrunch); total funding ~$41.6M per Crunchbase. ARR reported ~$50M (up from $7M start of 2025), with later reports citing $100M ARR. Core business is a general human-data/AI-recruiting platform (Scale AI competitor, AI recruiter "Zara"); robotics is a dedicated data product (micro1.ai/data-engine/robotics): expert-demonstrated tasks, multi-view teleoperation/POV trajectories from humanoids, and fine-grained action segmentation for VLA/imitation learning. In scope as a teleoperation/demonstration data service for embodied AI (L3 Data Infrastructure), though robotics is a division of the broader data-engine company. Clients include Microsoft and Fortune 100 firms. Team size not disclosed.
business_model: Sells human-generated training data to AI labs via contract/services deals: domain experts (vetted by its AI recruiter "Zara") produce data labeling, expert demonstrations, and—through its Robotics Data Engine—teleoperation trajectories, POV/multi-view recordings, and action labeling (JSONL/CSV) for VLA and imitation learning. Revenue comes from per-project/recurring data-delivery contracts with frontier AI and robotics labs plus enterprises; experts are paid contractors (including paid smart-glasses data capture).
customers: CEO Ali Ansari says it works with leading AI labs including Microsoft and several Fortune 100 companies; specific robotics-lab customers are not publicly named.
revenue_status: scaling (crossed $100M ARR Dec 2025, up from ~$7M ARR at start of 2025; raised $35M Series A at $500M valuation)
technical_differentiation: Differentiates from data-labeling rivals Scale AI, Mercor, and Surge via "Zara," an AI-led recruiting/vetting engine that sources and skill-tests domain experts (e.g., Stanford/Harvard professors) at scale, plus a dedicated robotics human-data pipeline (teleoperation + expert demonstrations for VLA/imitation learning). Still substantially smaller than Mercor (~$450M ARR) and Surge (~$1.2B), competing largely on execution, recruiting speed, and capturing demand shifting away from Meta-owned Scale AI.

one_liner: Provides end-to-end robotics demonstration-data pipelines (egocentric capture, human manipulation demos, labeling) plus broad data annotation, supplying training data for robot learning and physical AI.
notes: Founded 2018 (company About page); some sources say 2019 (Crunchbase) with founders James McLauchlin and Ravishankar Rajalingam. HQ at 20715 N Pima Rd, Scottsdale, AZ (also offices in Phoenix/Mesa AZ and India). Appears bootstrapped/self-funded; Crunchbase lists no disclosed funding rounds, hence funding fields undisclosed/unknown. Team size 2,000+ per company site (third-party sources estimate ~1,500). Borderline scope: parent is a broad data-services/annotation firm, but it runs a specific robotics demonstration-data product (egocentric capture, manipulation demos like clothing folding, contracted by data platforms such as Encord) which is a teleoperation/demonstration data service => in_scope=true. Primary layer L3 Data Infrastructure. Sources: objectways.com robotics/egocentric blogs, about-us page, Crunchbase, Tracxn.
business_model: Services-based data vendor: sells human-in-the-loop data annotation and end-to-end robotics demonstration-data pipelines (egocentric/teleoperation capture, human manipulation demos, motion labeling, 3D point cloud/LiDAR annotation) on a project/contract basis to AI and robotics teams, billed per-project or per-labeled-unit (managed workforce of ~1,600-2,000 specialists). Pricing/contract terms are not publicly disclosed.
customers: Largely undisclosed; case studies reference an unnamed "U.S. robotics client" training humanoid robots (200 towel-folding demos), Fortune 500 enterprises, autonomous-vehicle startups, and Thomson Reuters, but specific paying robotics customers are not publicly named.
revenue_status: scaling (est. $25M-$50M revenue range, unverified third-party estimate)
technical_differentiation: Differentiates on a large in-house global specialist workforce (~1,600-2,000) and full-pipeline egocentric/teleoperation capture-through-labeling for physical-AI/robotics, vs platform-first competitors like Scale AI and Encord (tooling-led) and Shaip/Labellerr (annotation-services peers); positioning is execution- and labor-capacity-driven rather than a proprietary technology moat.

one_liner: Builds dexterous robotic hands and the Data Collection Glove C1 (stretchable sensors, 120Hz, 1mm precision) to teleoperate robots and collect hand-motion demonstration data for training.
notes: Pure-play teleoperation-hardware-for-training-data company. Builds dexterous hands plus the Data Collection Glove C1 (stretchable elastic sensors, 120Hz, 1mm precision, 0.1deg resolution, ROS2/SteamVR/C++ SDK) explicitly for teleoperation and collecting hand-motion demonstration data to train embodied-intelligence robot policies. Maps to L3 Data Infrastructure (demonstration-data collection), with L4 hardware (gloves/hands) and L6 robot products (R1D1). AI-native and in-scope: the demonstration-data/teleop category exists to train learned manipulation policies. Founded 2024, HQ Mountain View CA per Tracxn/Crunchbase. Founders: Abhishek Gupta (CEO), Jackson Jesionowski, Ashish Gupta; site lists Jack Jay as founder. Investor Persist Ventures; total funding and round size/date undisclosed. Team ~16+ per company site (engineering team expanding). Sources: opendroids.com, Crunchbase, Tracxn.
business_model: Hardware sales of robotics products: the R1D1 mobile manipulator robot (~$18,500), the DH116 dexterous hand, and the Data Collection Glove C1 ($3,000/unit, sold via $100 refundable pre-order deposit) for teleoperation and hand-motion data capture. Buyers are robotics/AI research teams, academic institutions, and service/hospitality operators; also positions as a custom robotics integrator (project-based contracts).
customers: Reported to be in pre-order talks with Baskin-Robbins and Subway for service robots (per The Robot Report); targets robotics R&D labs, AI/ML teams, and universities. No confirmed paying customers publicly named - largely undisclosed.
revenue_status: early-revenue (no public revenue/ARR figure disclosed; product sales via pre-orders, seed-funded, founded 2024)
technical_differentiation: Differentiates the Glove C1 via stretchable elastic sensors (0-50% stretch range, 1mm/0.1deg precision, 120Hz, 40g) at a low $3,000 price versus pricier rigid-IMU/exoskeleton data gloves; pairs glove with its own DH116 dexterous hand and low-cost open-source R1D1 robot. Closest named competitors: Weave Robotics (home humanoid) and lower-cost platforms vs Unitree on the robot side; on data-glove side competes with exoskeleton/IMU capture rigs - competing largely on affordability and an integrated glove-to-hand-to-robot stack.

one_liner: Builds a 'human intelligence layer' for robots: surface-EMG sensors plus PULSE foundation model reconstruct hand posture and force/tactile data, supplying embodied-AI training data without making hardware.
notes: Beijing embodied-AI data-infrastructure startup founded 2025 by 25-year-old Tsinghua PhD Qin Shendao. Raised CNY 500M+ (~$73M) across angel, strategic, and Pre-A1 rounds in five months; most recent Pre-A1 led by Monolith Capital with follow-on from Puhua Capital.
